records.ety: [OE. hurtlen, freq. of hurten. See Hurt, v. t., and cf. Hurl.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hurtled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Hurtling (?).]

defs: To meet with violence or shock; to clash; to jostle.
To move rapidly; to wheel or rush suddenly or with violence; to whirl round rapidly; to skirmish.
quotes: Now hurtling round, advantage for to take.
Down the hurtling cataract of the ages.

defs: To make a threatening sound, like the clash of arms; to make a sound as of confused clashing or confusion; to resound.
quotes: The noise of battle hurtled in the air.
The earthquake sound Hurtling 'death the solid ground.

defs: To move with violence or impetuosity; to whirl; to brandish.
quotes: His harmful club he gan to hurtle high.

defs: To push; to jostle; to hurl.
quotes: And he hurtleth with his horse adown.
